zh.Paull, Kingston upon Hull, Yorkshire, En: 英國英格蘭越可爾金斯頓 武朋 赫爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當William Holme Of Paull Holme於1380年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾霍爾梅時，他的父親Robert Holme of Paull Holme36歲，母親Julian Rockley26歲。他於1403年與Joan Marflet在英國英格蘭越可爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1408年在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾巴辛波享年28歲，埋葬在英國越可爾霍爾梅。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Holme Of Paull Holme于1380年出生在英国英格兰越可爾霍爾梅时，他的父亲Robert Holme of Paull Holme36岁，母亲Julian Rockley26岁。他于1403年与Joan Marflet在英国英格兰越可爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1408年在英国英格兰坎布利其爾巴辛波享年28岁，埋葬在英国越可爾霍爾梅。

nameMeanings.name: Holme
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik.
English (northwestern England): variant of   Holm   and of   Home   3 and 4.
Danish and Swedish: variant of   Holm   1.

variants.name: Holm
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Anders, Thor, Holger, Nels, Nils, Sven, Helmer, Lennart, Niels, Vibeke.

English, Scottish, German, and Scandinavian: topographic name for someone who lived on an island, in particular a piece of slightly raised land lying in a fen or partly surrounded by streams, from Middle English, Middle Low German holm, Old Norse holmr, or else a habitational name from a place called with this element. The Swedish name is often ornamental.

English: topographic name for someone who lived where holly grew, from Middle English holm, a divergent development of Old English hole(g)n ‘holly (tree)’; or, especially in southern England, a habitational name from any of the places such as East and West Holme (Dorset) or Holne (Devon). Compare Hollis .

variants.name: Holmes
meanings: English: either from the plural form of Holme , or else a variant of Holme or Home , with excrescent -s (see Holm ).

Scottish: probably a habitational name from Holmes near Dundonald (Ayrshire), or possibly from another place so called in the barony of Inchestuir (Angus). Both placenames likely derive from the plural form of Middle English, Older Scots holm ‘islet, raised land in a marsh’ (see Holm ).

Scottish and Irish: adopted for Scottish Gaelic and Irish Mac Thómais, Mac Thómais (see McComb ). In parts of western Ireland, Holmes is also a variant of Cavish, from Gaelic Mac Thámhais, another patronymic from Thomas . Early bearers in Ireland were probably immigrants from Scotland.

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: Paull
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and German: variant of Paul . The surname Paull is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: Holme
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik.
English (northwestern England): variant of   Holm   and of   Home   3 and 4.
Danish and Swedish: variant of   Holm   1.

variants.name: Holm
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Anders, Thor, Holger, Nels, Nils, Sven, Helmer, Lennart, Niels, Vibeke.

English, Scottish, German, and Scandinavian: topographic name for someone who lived on an island, in particular a piece of slightly raised land lying in a fen or partly surrounded by streams, from Middle English, Middle Low German holm, Old Norse holmr, or else a habitational name from a place called with this element. The Swedish name is often ornamental.

English: topographic name for someone who lived where holly grew, from Middle English holm, a divergent development of Old English hole(g)n ‘holly (tree)’; or, especially in southern England, a habitational name from any of the places such as East and West Holme (Dorset) or Holne (Devon). Compare Hollis .

variants.name: Holmes
meanings: English: either from the plural form of Holme , or else a variant of Holme or Home , with excrescent -s (see Holm ).

Scottish: probably a habitational name from Holmes near Dundonald (Ayrshire), or possibly from another place so called in the barony of Inchestuir (Angus). Both placenames likely derive from the plural form of Middle English, Older Scots holm ‘islet, raised land in a marsh’ (see Holm ).

Scottish and Irish: adopted for Scottish Gaelic and Irish Mac Thómais, Mac Thómais (see McComb ). In parts of western Ireland, Holmes is also a variant of Cavish, from Gaelic Mac Thámhais, another patronymic from Thomas . Early bearers in Ireland were probably immigrants from Scotland.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When William Holme Of Paull Holme was born in 1380, in Holme, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert Holme of Paull Holme, was 36 and his mother, Julian Rockley, was 26. He married Joan Marflet in 1403, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1408, in Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 28, and was buried in Holme, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Holme Of Paull Holme est né en 1380, à Holme, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert Holme of Paull Holme, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Julian Rockley, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Joan Marflet en 1403, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1408, à Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 28 ans, et a été enterré à Holme, Everwyck, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: William Holme Of Paull Holme(이)가 1380년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Holme of Paull Holme(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Julian Rockley(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1403년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 Joan Marflet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1408년 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 배징보우른에서 28세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 홀에 묻혔다.

ru.Paull, Kingston upon Hull, Yorkshire, En: кингстон упон хулл, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Holme Of Paull Holme родился в 1380 г. в местности холм, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Robert Holme of Paull Holme было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Julian Rockley, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Marflet в 1403 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1408 г. в местности бассингбурн, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 28 лет и был похоронен в местности холм, еркшир, Англия.

Se casó con Joan Marflet en 1403, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1408, en Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 28 años, y fue sepultado en Holme, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
